  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I limit improvements to sentence-level clarity and concision without changing structure or claim strength (Option A), or also reorganize and reframe the passage while softening or substantiating strong claims like “first,” “strongest,” and “deployment-ready” (Option B)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Improvement may require clarifying the conceptual positioning and contribution claims (e.g., what is novel about the combined strategy).",
    "R1.5": "Enhancement could include reorganizing paragraphs to create a tighter narrative from LP planning to refinement and dispatch.",
    "R1.6": "The primary need is polishing wording, reducing redundancy, and improving sentence- and paragraph-level flow.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguities (e.g., scope of ‘first’ and guarantees under rolling re-planning) will be noted rather than assumed away.",
    "RX.2": "We will avoid amplifying unverified superlatives and ensure claims are stated conservatively.",
    "RX.3": "Work will stay within improving the provided passage without adding new experiments, citations, or external content.",
    "RX.4": "Edits will avoid overstating deployment readiness or guarantees that could mislead downstream users.",
    "RX.5": "Terminology from the draft will be preserved and any changes will remain traceable to the original phrasing."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I limit improvements to sentence-level clarity and concision without changing structure or claim strength (Option A), or also reorganize and reframe the passage while softening or substantiating strong claims like “first,” “strongest,” and “deployment-ready” (Option B)?

rationale.R1.1: Improvement may require clarifying the conceptual positioning and contribution claims (e.g., what is novel about the combined strategy).
rationale.R1.5: Enhancement could include reorganizing paragraphs to create a tighter narrative from LP planning to refinement and dispatch.
rationale.R1.6: The primary need is polishing wording, reducing redundancy, and improving sentence- and paragraph-level flow.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguities (e.g., scope of ‘first’ and guarantees under rolling re-planning) will be noted rather than assumed away.
rationale.RX.2: We will avoid amplifying unverified superlatives and ensure claims are stated conservatively.
rationale.RX.3: Work will stay within improving the provided passage without adding new experiments, citations, or external content.
rationale.RX.4: Edits will avoid overstating deployment readiness or guarantees that could mislead downstream users.
rationale.RX.5: Terminology from the draft will be preserved and any changes will remain traceable to the original phrasing.
